active_goal: Improve MLX-engine inference performance until further optimization no longer produces promotion-worthy end-to-end gains, while preserving response quality, stability, and reproducibility.

completed_tasks.name: VLM persistent prompt-cache restore improvements
completed_tasks.summary: Persistent cache, exact prepared-prompt metadata restore, cached image text-suffix fast path, adaptive restore chunk sizing, and path-based safetensor mx.load are retained.

completed_tasks.name: Response-quality gate
completed_tasks.summary: Path-load repeat quality compare passed; faster unsafe candidates were rejected when quality, decode stability, or runtime stability failed.

completed_tasks.name: Restore-path profiling
completed_tasks.summary: KV-delta load was reduced from 21.052 ms to 1.546-1.861 ms; remaining restore cost is dominated by mx.eval materialization.

completed_tasks.name: Persistent VLM record-layout candidate
completed_tasks.summary: Full-prefix KV spans were rejected after persistent-cache regression; redundant current-only KV records are now skipped when a two-chunk KV span is saved, with repeated quality-passing latency improvement.

completed_tasks.name: Batched text startup warmup hang fix
completed_tasks.summary: BatchedModelKit now signals startup readiness after model load and generation-stream preparation instead of waiting for all startup warmup work. The expensive 7,162-token benchmark-shape warmup is opt-in via MLX_ENGINE_STARTUP_LONG_WARMUP=1, and warmup stops early when a user request is queued.

remaining_tasks.name: Package retained changes into reviewable branch slice
remaining_tasks.notes: Retained code changes are committed on mlx-vlm-prompt-cache-perf. Benchmark harness edits live in /Users/jeffreycruz/Development/LLM_INFERENCE/mlx-bench-harness, which is not a git repo.

remaining_tasks.name: Investigate restore-time mx.eval materialization
remaining_tasks.notes: Eager per-record materialization was rejected after two quality-passing runs stayed below the 3% promotion threshold and decode TPS regressed. Reverted in commit 6a1fae0.

remaining_tasks.name: Broaden model and workload coverage
remaining_tasks.notes: Dense/code Qwen2.5-Coder-14B passed the deterministic text-quality gate. MoE Qwen3.6-35B-A3B ran successfully but failed quality because it emitted visible thinking text across the suite.

remaining_tasks.name: Persistent VLM record-layout candidate
remaining_tasks.notes: Implemented retained lower-write-amplification slice in commit 859f7c4: chunks that save a two-chunk KV span no longer also persist the redundant current-only KV record. Full-prefix KV span packing was rejected and documented in commit 8ad603a.

remaining_tasks.name: Continue restore eval/materialization investigation
remaining_tasks.notes: Next implementation target: reduce retained warm-restore eval_ms without removing the restore-time mx.eval safety barrier. Current timing profile shows records=5, load_chunks_ms=1.299, assemble_ms=0.035, eval_ms=4.258, warm_ttft_s=0.017111.

remaining_tasks.name: Push branch for storage
remaining_tasks.notes: Branch mlx-vlm-prompt-cache-perf is pushed to origin. Review package written to /Users/jeffreycruz/Development/LLM_INFERENCE/mlx-engine/.planning/review-package-20260620.md. Promotion pytest group passed after the startup warmup fix. No PR should remain open at this stage.

decisions.rationale: Removing it failed the warm restore path with RuntimeError: There is no Stream(gpu, 3) in current thread.
It reduced restore time but failed the response-quality/decode stability gate twice.
Quality passed, but TTFT, total latency, and decode TPS were worse than the retained path.
It preserved quality and reduced KV-delta record-load time by roughly 91-93%.

decisions.decision: Add deterministic text-quality profile before further text-path promotion
decisions.rationale: The task-diverse text suite showed strong performance improvement but failed one quality check because both baseline and candidate emit thinking text and hit token_limit on instruction_format. The deterministic profile adds prompt-specific caps, forbidden visible-thinking checks, and exact JSON-key validation.

decisions.decision: Target persistent VLM record layout next
decisions.rationale: Dense/code validation passed, MoE validation is quality-blocked by model behavior, and unsafe mx.eval removal already produced cross-thread stream failures. The next likely performance win is reducing restore assembly/materialization cost without removing the safety barrier.
Functional tests passed, but the fair persistent-cache benchmark failed: TTFT +7.786%, decode TPS -33.500%, total latency +8.972% versus retained baseline.

decisions.decision: Retain redundant current-only KV record skip
decisions.rationale: The restore planner already prefers the two-chunk KV span. Skipping the redundant current-only physical record preserved quality and repeated with TTFT -3.013% and total latency -2.566% versus retained baseline.

decisions.decision: Make exact long-prompt startup warmup opt-in
decisions.rationale: Mandatory 7,162-token startup warmup made large-model BatchedModelKit startup look hung and blocked the prefill test for minutes. Bounded warmup remains default; the long benchmark shape can still be enabled explicitly with MLX_ENGINE_STARTUP_LONG_WARMUP=1.

blockers.description: Earlier runner SIGKILL was localized to the default .venv interpreter. Use .venv-py312 for shared_bench.py reruns.
blockers.workaround: Pass --mlx-engine-python /Users/jeffreycruz/Development/LLM_INFERENCE/mlx-engine/.venv-py312/bin/python. Avoid inline env assignments in this Codex tool session until Metal initialization behavior is rechecked.
